message:
test(ft-hfbsp): comprehensive simd_scan harness + multi-split + adversarial corpus

simd_scan::scan_newlines_and_ansi runs against every byte of every
captured pane delta — including bytes from agent processes whose
output is treated as untrusted. SIMD code is a known UB hotspot
(chunk-tail off-by-one, integer overflow on adversarial ANSI
parameters, alignment crashes on stricter targets, quadratic blowup
on `\x1b[\x1b[\x1b[…` recursive escape introducer prefixes).

## Files
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/simd_scan.rs (newly tracked; was untracked from
  prior agent work). Pins four contracts:
  1. SIMD vs scalar reference equivalence (the main crash-detector).
  2. logical_line_count consistent with newline_count + final-\\n
     edge case.
  3. ansi_density finite + bounded to [0.0, 1.0].
  4. Chunk-stitching equivalence — original single-split coverage
     PLUS new MULTI-SPLIT mode driven by an additional Arbitrary
     `multi_split_seeds` byte slice. Up to 8 chunk boundaries per
     input, ANSI state machine carried forward across all chunks via
     a single OutputScanState (the production streaming model).
- fuzz/corpus/simd_scan/ — 10 seeds chosen for SIMD edge-case
  triggering: csi_basic, osc_then_csi, dcs_st_terminator,
  recursive_csi_intro, integer_overflow_param,
  long_osc_payload, plain_newlines, empty, lone_esc, csi_intro_only.

## Verified
- cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin simd_scan → exit 0,
  1.4s incremental.

The multi-split mode is the material extension over the original
single-split coverage. Chunk-tail bugs in SIMD scanners typically
hide at chunk boundaries; up to 8 boundaries per fuzzer input lets
libFuzzer minimize toward inputs that hit the tail-loop with state
carried across multiple cuts. Reuses ONE OutputScanState across all
chunks so the ANSI state machine cross-boundary semantics get
exercised.

Pattern reuse: matches the harness shape from ft-h8v8v
(wire_envelope) + ipc_auth_envelope — Archetype 5 structure-aware
Arbitrary input + Archetype 1 crash detector with SIMD/scalar
differential as the primary oracle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
